articles.title: IMD issue heatwave alert for Rajasthan: AC for animals installed at Gujarat's Sardar Patel Zoological Park;... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Amravati-Wardha hottest in India as mercury hits 46°C:Animals at Gujarat’s Ekta Nagar zoological park cool off with ACs, ice blocks placed at Vadodara zoo
Eight states like Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra-Vidarbha in the west to Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh, are reeling under intense heat. Temperatures in these areas are consistently exceeding 40°C. Along with the days, nights are also getting hotter.
Amravati and Wardha in Maharashtra were the hottest cities in the country on Saturday. Both cities recorded temperatures of 46°C. Banda in Uttar Pradesh, Naogaon in Madhya Pradesh, and Khandwa also recorded temperatures above 44°C.
Meanwhile, ACs, coolers, and sprinklers were installed for animals and birds at the Jungle Safari near the Statue of Unity in Ekta Nagar, Gujarat, at Sardar Patel Zoological Park.
Vadodara's Sayajibagh Zoological Park has implemented several cooling measures to protect animals and birds from the heat, including water sprinklers, ice cubes, and foggers.
The Indian Meteorological Department has issued an alert for a heatwave and temperatures reaching up to 45°C in several areas on Sunday, as severe heat is set to return to Rajasthan
Amravati and Wardha in Maharashtra were the hottest cities with temperatures reaching up to 46 °C. Meanwhile, Yavatmal and Brahmapuri of Maharashtra recorded 45.2°C.
In Uttar Pradesh, Banda was the hottest city in the state on Saturday, where the maximum temperature was recorded at 44.8°C.
Arrangements for cold water and shade have also been made for tourists. The weather in Phalodi recorded the highest temperature in the state on Saturday at 44.8°C.
Next two days' weather forecast
May 18:
- Alert for rain and lightning in Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Chhattisgarh, and northeastern states.
- Severe heatwave alert in Rajasthan. Nights will also remain hot. Heatwave will also prevail in Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Delhi, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, and Maharashtra.
- Heavy rainfall is predicted in Assam, Meghalaya, Kerala, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu.
May 19:
- Severe heatwave conditions are likely at isolated places over Uttar Pradesh. Heatwave conditions are also likely at isolated places over Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Rajasthan, and Maharashtra.
- Strong winds with speeds of 30-40 kmph are likely at isolated places over Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Puducherry.
- Thunderstorms with strong winds (50-60 kmph) are likely at isolated places over Jharkhand. Lightning is likely over Arunachal Pradesh.
3 Weather Snapshots Across the Country
In Prayagraj, children riding on a scooter were seen covering their faces with their hands and clothes due to the heatwave.
In Bikaner, a traffic policeman was seen performing duty, covering his face with a gamcha to escape the heat.
In Jaipur, water was sprayed on the roads by the municipal corporation's anti-smog gun.
